text.title: AWS Monitoring, Alerting, and Disaster Recovery Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules grounded in AWS Well-Architected (Reliability + OpEx) + real-world DR drills.

rule.statement: Every prod service MUST have a CloudWatch dashboard covering: API request+error rate, compute metrics (Lambda dur/err/throttle OR ECS/EKS CPU/mem), DB consumed capacity, queue depth. 5-min aggregation; 3-h default window.
rule.rationale: Consistent time windows across widgets let engineers correlate spikes across tiers in one view. 5-min smooths noise while staying granular.

rule.statement: Alarms MUST be tiered into critical (page), warning (business-hours notify), informational (daily digest). Single 'alert' channel is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Untiered alerts cause alarm fatigue. Tiered routing makes critical pages survive while non-critical noise stays in a digest.

rule.statement: Lambda + API Gateway prod functions MUST have X-Ray tracing = Active. Service-map MUST cover the critical request path.
rule.rationale: Without X-Ray, multi-service request traces require log correlation by hand; MTTR balloons. Active tracing samples 5% baseline + 100% of errors.

rule.statement: DR strategy MUST match RTO: backup-restore (RTO >24h), pilot-light (RTO 1-24h), warm-standby (RTO <1h), active-active (RTO seconds). Cheaper strategy for tighter RTO is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Backup-restore with RTO of 1h is impossible — restore alone takes longer. Mismatched strategy is a paper plan that fails on the day.

rule.statement: DR drills MUST run at least quarterly. The drill MUST be from cold (kill the primary, restore from DR plan); paper walkthroughs do not count.
rule.rationale: Untested DR is no DR. Drills find the assumption nobody wrote down — every time.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
